MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-22-2021 Vacated

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-07-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-07-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. LORI E. ANDRUS, Esq. and AUDREY SIEGEL, Esq. of Andrus Anderson LLP (Tel: 415-986-1400) for plaintiff Maria Martinez;
Atty. KARYN N. CHANDLER, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith (Tel: 415-362-2580) for defendant Lyft, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Counsel for both sides are to confer on a proposed order which largely tracks the tentative ruling but adds language that the deposition is to occur no later than September 14, 2021 on a date agreed to by the parties. Counsel may add proposed language agreed upon by the parties setting some limitations as to Topic 8. Counsel are to email the proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Plaintiff Maria Martinez Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Requests (Part 2 Of 2 Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by Counsel. Court Reporter N. Presant was present. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA MARTINEZ's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Requests.

The plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production numbers 24, 28, 29, 30, 31 and 43 are granted. The requests regarding prior accidents and driving incidents are relevant as to the claim of negligent hiring, retention, training and supervision. The responses to date reveal that there were prior incidents. Further documents likely exist including photographs, damage estimates, incident reports, claims history etc. The objections asserting attorney client and work product were timely and properly asserted and those have not been waived. However, if documents exist which fall within those privileges, they must be identified in a privilege log. All other objections based on privacy or relevance are overruled as the documents are relevant and any claim of privacy is overridden by their relevance. The amended, verified responses along with a complete set of documents responsive to the requests is due no later than 10 days from the date of the ruling on this matter. If documents are withheld due to the attorney client or work product privileges, a privilege log is also due within 10 days of the date of the ruling on this matter.

The motion with respect to request 43 is also granted. Social media posts within the time frame of one hour before and one hour after the incident must be produced without objection. The amended, verified responses along with a complete set of documents responsive to the request is due no later than 10 days from the date of the ruling on this matter.

The motion with respect to requests 35 and 36 is moot as the plaintiff stated her willingness to withdraw those requests for the black box information. Such a withdrawal in this case is appropriate as the benefit of that information is outweighed by the cost and complexity of obtaining that information, if any.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
